sourceMovie.title: The Thieves
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Thieves for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Choi Dong-hoon keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Red Notice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Thieves clicked for you, Red Notice (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Italian Job
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Thieves clicked for you, The Italian Job (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Man on a Ledge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Thieves clicked for you, Man on a Ledge (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Thieves?
faq.answer: Try Red Notice, The Italian Job, Man on a Ledge—each shares DNA with The Thieves while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Thieves?
faq.answer: The Thieves (2012) is best described as Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Thieves worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
